info.title: TBBO API
info.description:
Authentication uses short-lived session tokens (3 hours).

Send the token with every request using the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Token <token>
```

Example:

```
Authorization: Token 123abc456def789
```

To obtain a token, call `POST /auth/login/` with valid credentials. The response includes a `key` value. Use that value in the header above for subsequent API calls.

How to find required IDs:

- `challenge_id`: Prop Tech Configuration -> Challenges
- `user_id`: Tickblaze Manager -> Users
- `account_id`: Tickblaze Manager -> Accounts

post.description: Create a new trading account for a user. Starting balance precedence is: explicit account `initial_balance`, then the selected Phase `initial_balance`, then the Challenge `initial_balance`. When `phase_id` is omitted or null, the account uses the first phase for `challenge_id`; provide `phase_id` to create the account for a specific phase in that challenge.

BasicTradingAccount.summary: Create a basic trading account using the challenge default balance
BasicTradingAccount.description: Example of creating a standard trading account with minimal required fields

AdvancedTradingAccount.summary: Create an account with an explicit balance override
AdvancedTradingAccount.description: Example with custom symbols, position limits, notes, and an explicit starting balance.

SpecificPhaseTradingAccount.summary: Create an account for a specific challenge phase
SpecificPhaseTradingAccount.description: Example of creating an account using an exact phase id while keeping challenge_id for compatibility.

value.notes: Account created for testing purposes. Will be activated after verification.

InactiveAccount.description: Example of creating an account that starts in inactive state

results.notes: Passed Phase 1 and Phase 2 evaluation. Ready for live trading with full symbol access.

SuccessResponse.description: Response when account is successfully created

get.description: Returns information for a specific trading account.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A unique integer value identifying this account.

patch.description: Deactivate a trading account with a reason and, when requested, submit close-position requests for its open positions.

DeactivateWithoutClosingPositions.description: Example of deactivating an account while leaving open positions unchanged

DeactivateAndClosePositions.summary: Deactivate account and submit close-position requests
DeactivateAndClosePositions.description: Example of deactivating an account and submitting close-position requests

value.reason: Maximum drawdown limit exceeded. Account violated risk management rules.

DeactivateForRuleViolation.description: Example of deactivating an account due to rule violation and submitting close-position requests

value.reason: Account inactive for 90+ days. Deactivating per policy.

DeactivateForInactivity.description: Example of deactivating an account due to prolonged inactivity

value.reason: Failed Phase 1 evaluation. Daily loss limit breached on day 3.

DeactivateForFailedEvaluation.description: Example of deactivating an account that failed the evaluation phase

SuccessResponse-PositionsNotClosed.description: Response when account is deactivated but positions remain open

results.failure_reason: Maximum drawdown limit exceeded. Account violated risk management rules.

SuccessResponse-PositionsClosed.summary: Successful deactivation with close requests submitted
SuccessResponse-PositionsClosed.description: Response when the account is deactivated and all required close-position requests were accepted

patch.description: Reactivate a previously deactivated account with an optional balance reset.

value.reason: User requested reactivation after temporary suspension

ReactivateWithoutReset.description: Example of reactivating an account while maintaining the existing balance

value.reason: Account failed evaluation. User purchased new challenge to retry.

ReactivateWithBalanceReset.description: Example of reactivating an account and resetting balance to initial amount (fresh start)

value.reason: Reactivating after platform maintenance. No balance reset needed.

ReactivateAfterTechnicalIssue.description: Example of reactivating an account that was deactivated due to technical issues

value.reason: Trader violated max drawdown rule. Offering fresh start with reset balance after review.

ReactivateAfterRuleViolation.description: Example of giving trader a second chance with reset balance

SuccessResponse-WithoutReset.description: Response when account is reactivated without resetting balance

SuccessResponse-WithReset.description: Response when account is reactivated and balance is reset to initial amount

patch.description: Adjust account balances in bulk. Supports add, subtract, and set operations for one or multiple accounts.

AddBalance(Bulk).description: Adds the specified amount to the balance of each account. Useful for profit sharing, bonuses, or balance corrections.

SubtractBalance(Bulk).description: Subtracts the specified amount from each account balance. Used for fees, penalties, or balance corrections. Accounts cannot end with a negative balance.

SetBalance(Bulk).description: Sets the balance of all provided accounts to the exact same value. Use this operation to set each provided account to the same balance.

SuccessResponse-Add.description: Response when balances are successfully increased for multiple accounts.

SuccessResponse-Subtract.description: Response when balances are successfully decreased for multiple accounts.

SuccessResponse-Set.description: Response when balances are set to a fixed value for multiple accounts.

get.description: Returns paginated accounts for end-of-day reporting. By default, returns only `ACTIVE` accounts. Pass `?status=FAILED`, another AccountStatusChoices value, or `?status=all` to broaden the result set. Combine with `?disabled_at_after=` and `?disabled_at_before=` to scope results to a window of when the account entered its terminal state (max window: 31 days; useful for daily failed-account roll-ups). Staff users see all accessible accounts; regular users see only their own.
get.summary: Get balance, equity and reporting fields for accounts

parameters.name: disabled_at_after
parameters.description: Return only accounts whose disabled timestamp is at or after this ISO 8601 datetime. Combine `*_after` and `*_before` to define a window. The maximum window span is 31 days when both bounds are set.

parameters.name: disabled_at_before
parameters.description: Return only accounts whose disabled timestamp is at or before this ISO 8601 datetime. Combine `*_after` and `*_before` to define a window. The maximum window span is 31 days when both bounds are set.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter accounts by status. Accepts any AccountStatusChoices value (e.g. ACTIVE, FAILED, BLOCKED) or the literal 'all' to disable filtering. Defaults to ACTIVE when omitted.

SuccessResponse-ActiveAccount.description: Default response shape for an ACTIVE account.

SuccessResponse-FailedAccount.description: Returned when filtering with `?status=FAILED` (or `?status=all`). `status_reason` carries the enum needed to detect a daily-loss-limit soft breach without an extra per-account call.

SuccessResponse-Empty.description: Response when no accounts match the filter.

get.description: Returns currently disabled accounts. By default the endpoint returns accounts disabled within the rolling last 24 hours. Callers may pass `start_date` and `end_date` in `YYYY-MM-DD` to request an inclusive range up to 31 days.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: Inclusive end date in `YYYY-MM-DD`. Defaults to the rolling last 24 hours when omitted.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for paginated responses.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of records per page.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Inclusive start date in `YYYY-MM-DD`. Defaults to the rolling last 24 hours when omitted.

results.reason: Daily drawdown breached during London session

200.description: Paginated list of currently disabled accounts ordered by most recent disable timestamp.

post.description: Reactivate failed accounts so their traders can resume trading. Each account in `account_ids` returns to ACTIVE and its failure reason is cleared. Accounts that aren't currently failed are reported under `skipped` instead of rejecting the whole request, so retries are safe. Set `reset_balance` to restore balance and equity to the account's initial balance. Maximum 500 accounts per request.

Soft-breachEODReset.description: Reactivate accounts that failed today for DAILY_DRAWDOWN_EXCEEDED so traders resume trading next session. Balance is preserved.

FreshStartWithBalanceReset.description: Reactivate accounts and reset balance/equity to the initial balance.

Partial-skipResponse.description: Account 1003 was already ACTIVE (e.g. a previous retry succeeded). It is skipped, not failed.

patch.description: Updates the account status for every account in `account_ids` and stores the optional `custom_status_reason`. Returns a payload summarizing how many account statuses changed.

get.description: Returns paginated accounts with optional field selection through `query`. Callers can request account fields, `user` and `phase` objects, position counts, and up to 10 recent closed positions per account. `status` filters accounts and defaults to `ACTIVE`; use `all` to disable that filter. `position_counts_closed_at_after` and `position_counts_closed_at_before` limit only the position count fields, not the returned accounts. Staff users see accessible firm accounts; regular users see only their own.
get.summary: List accounts with optional field selection and nested objects

parameters.name: broker_type
parameters.description: Filter accounts by broker type. Accepts a single value or a comma-separated list of values. Multiple values are treated as a union (return rows matching any of them).

parameters.name: created_after
parameters.description: Return only accounts created at or after this ISO 8601 datetime. Combine `*_after` and `*_before` to define a window. The maximum window span is 31 days when both bounds are set.

parameters.name: created_before
parameters.description: Return only accounts created at or before this ISO 8601 datetime. Combine `*_after` and `*_before` to define a window. The maximum window span is 31 days when both bounds are set.

parameters.name: disabled_at_after
parameters.description: Return only accounts whose disabled timestamp is at or after this ISO 8601 datetime. Combine `*_after` and `*_before` to define a window. The maximum window span is 31 days when both bounds are set.

parameters.name: disabled_at_before
parameters.description: Return only accounts whose disabled timestamp is at or before this ISO 8601 datetime. Combine `*_after` and `*_before` to define a window. The maximum window span is 31 days when both bounds are set.

parameters.name: modified_after
parameters.description: Return only accounts last modified at or after this ISO 8601 datetime. Useful for incremental syncs. Combine `*_after` and `*_before` to define a window. The maximum window span is 31 days when both bounds are set.

parameters.name: modified_before
parameters.description: Return only accounts last modified at or before this ISO 8601 datetime. Combine `*_after` and `*_before` to define a window. The maximum window span is 31 days when both bounds are set.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: 1-indexed page number. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Rows per page. Defaults to 20, max 100.

parameters.name: payout_frequency
parameters.description: Filter accounts by payout frequency. Accepts a single value or a comma-separated list of values. Multiple values are treated as a union (return rows matching any of them).

parameters.name: position_counts_closed_at_after
parameters.description: Scope `long_positions_count` and `short_positions_count` to closed positions with close timestamps at or after this ISO 8601 datetime. This changes only those count fields; it does not change which accounts are returned. Counts are all-time when this and `position_counts_closed_at_before` are omitted.

parameters.name: position_counts_closed_at_before
parameters.description: Scope `long_positions_count` and `short_positions_count` to closed positions with close timestamps at or before this ISO 8601 datetime. This count window is inclusive and based on closed-position close time.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
**Choose which fields you want in the response.**

Pass a brace-delimited list of the fields you want. The response includes exactly those fields, in the shape you request — useful for trimming large responses to just what your report needs, and for expanding related objects (user, firm, positions) inline so you don't need follow-up calls.

**Syntax:**
- Flat field list: `?query={account_id, balance, equity}`
- Nested object: `?query={account_id, user{id, email}}`
- Multiple nested objects: `?query={account_id, user{id}, phase{id, title}}`

**Nesting limit:** 2 levels deep. Requests nested deeper are rejected with HTTP 400. The outermost `{...}` counts as level 1; each `field{...}` inside adds a level.

**Behavior when omitted:** the response includes every field documented in the `200` response schema. For best performance, pass `?query=` explicitly and ask for only the fields you need — some fields (counts, lists of related objects) are skipped from the response cost when not requested.

PickAFewFields.description: Returns just those three fields per row.

End-of-dayReportingShape.description: Status and financial fields in one call, no nested objects.

AddPositionCountsPerAccount.description: Per-account counts of long and short closed positions.

IncludeUserAndPhaseDetails.value: {account_id, user{id, email, full_name}, phase{id, title, step}}
IncludeUserAndPhaseDetails.description: Owner and phase objects returned inline with the account row.

IncludeUpTo10RecentClosedPositionsPerAcc.summary: Include up to 10 recent closed positions per account
IncludeUpTo10RecentClosedPositionsPerAcc.description: Returns the 10 most-recently-closed positions per account, ordered newest first by close time. The 10-per-account limit is fixed and cannot be raised by the client.

FullReportingShape—Replaces4SeparateCall.value: {account_id, status, balance, equity, closed_pnl_today, trading_days, long_positions_count, short_positions_count, user{id, email}, closed_positions{id, symbol, profit, close_time}}
FullReportingShape—Replaces4SeparateCall.summary: Full reporting shape — replaces 4 separate calls
FullReportingShape—Replaces4SeparateCall.description: Account status, financials, position counts, owner info, and recent positions in one paginated response.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Filter accounts by their creation source. Accepts a single value or a comma-separated list of values. Multiple values are treated as a union (return rows matching any of them).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter accounts by status. Accepts any AccountStatusChoices value (e.g. ACTIVE, FAILED, BLOCKED) or the literal `all` to disable filtering. Defaults to ACTIVE when omitted.

parameters.name: status_reason
parameters.description: Filter accounts by status reason. Useful in combination with `status=FAILED` to detect specific failure modes (e.g. soft breaches). Accepts a single value or a comma-separated list of values. Multiple values are treated as a union (return rows matching any of them).

End-of-dayReportingShape.summary: Status, financials, risk metrics, and counts in one call
End-of-dayReportingShape.description: Request: `?status=all&query={account_id, status, status_reason, closed_pnl_today, profit_loss, trading_days, initial_balance, balance, equity, leverage, mae, mfe, long_positions_count, short_positions_count}`. Add `&position_counts_closed_at_after=2026-05-03T13:30:00Z&position_counts_closed_at_before=2026-05-03T20:00:00Z` to scope only the count fields to a trading-session close-time window.

WithUserAndPhase.summary: Owner and phase objects nested in each row
WithUserAndPhase.description: Request: `?query={account_id, status, user{id, email, full_name}, phase{id, title, step, target_profit, daily_drawdown, maximum_drawdown}}`

WithRecentClosedPositions.summary: Up to 10 most-recently-closed positions per account
WithRecentClosedPositions.description:
Request: `?query={account_id, closed_positions{id, symbol, direction, profit, open_price, close_price, open_time, close_time}}`. Returns up to 10 positions per account, ordered newest first by close time. The 10-per-account limit is fixed.

`direction` is returned as `Long` or `Short`. `state` is returned as `Open` or `Closed` (always `Closed` here).

EmptyResult.description: Returned when no accounts match `?status=` (or you have no visible accounts).

get.description: Returns a paginated list of Challenges for your firm, including nested phases, allowed exchange limits, and individual symbol limits.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of user entitlement records for users associated with your firm. Each record includes the overall entitlement status and your firm's status for that entitlement, when available.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Items per page.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by overall entitlement status.

get.description: Returns entitlement records for the requested `user_id` when the user is associated with your firm. Each record includes the overall entitlement status and your firm's status for that entitlement, when available.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Trader user id whose entitlement records should be returned.

get.description: Returns liveness information for infrastructure monitors.

Returns the current platform outage state. `ok` means no outage is reported. `scheduled_outage` is returned during the daily 17:00–18:00 America/New_York maintenance window. `unscheduled_outage` is reserved for unplanned outage reporting.

ScheduledOutage.summary: Within the daily 17:00–18:00 America/New_York maintenance window

patch.description: Submits close requests for each open symbol exposure on the specified account. Staff users can operate on any account for the authenticated firm, while non-staff users are limited to their own accounts. The response reports submitted and failed symbol requests; a 200 response does not guarantee every symbol request succeeded. `triggered_by` is optional and defaults to authenticated user metadata when omitted.
patch.summary: Submit close requests for all open positions for an account

patch.description: Submits close requests for each open symbol exposure on the specified account. Staff users can operate on any account for the authenticated firm, while non-staff users are limited to their own accounts. The response reports submitted and failed symbol requests; a 200 response does not guarantee every symbol request succeeded. `triggered_by` is optional and defaults to authenticated user metadata when omitted.
patch.summary: Submit close requests for all open positions for an account

get.description:
Returns positions for trading accounts the caller can access, filtered by state.
Staff users can see every account in the firm, while non-staff users only see their own accounts.

`date_from` / `date_to` are coarse date filters. For timestamp-level windows, use `opened_at_after` / `opened_at_before` with `state=OPEN` or `closed_at_after` / `closed_at_before` with `state=CLOSED`.

Default state is OPEN. Results are ordered by open_time DESC then id DESC for OPEN positions, and close_time DESC then open_time DESC for CLOSED positions.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Optional account ID. Regular users may only request their own accounts.

parameters.name: closed_at_after
parameters.description: For `state=CLOSED` only. Filter by `close_time` at or after this ISO 8601 datetime. Inclusive.

parameters.name: closed_at_before
parameters.description: For `state=CLOSED` only. Filter by `close_time` at or before this ISO 8601 datetime. Inclusive.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filter by open or close date. Use `YYYY-MM-DD`; `DD-MM-YYYY` is also accepted for compatibility. For timestamp precision, use the `opened_at_*` or `closed_at_*` filters.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filter by open or close date. Use `YYYY-MM-DD`; `DD-MM-YYYY` is also accepted for compatibility. For timestamp precision, use the `opened_at_*` or `closed_at_*` filters.

parameters.name: opened_at_after
parameters.description: For `state=OPEN` only. Filter by `open_time` at or after this ISO 8601 datetime. Inclusive.

parameters.name: opened_at_before
parameters.description: For `state=OPEN` only. Filter by `open_time` at or before this ISO 8601 datetime. Inclusive.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: prop_firm
parameters.description: Optional firm slug. When supplied, results are limited to the caller's own accounts for that firm.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Filter positions by state

patch.description: Submits a close request for the specified account and symbol. Staff users can operate on any position for the authenticated firm, while non-staff users are limited to their own accounts. `triggered_by` is optional and defaults to authenticated user metadata when omitted.
patch.summary: Submit a close request for one account and symbol

get.description: Returns paginated users for your firm, including account counts.

parameters.name: customer_type
parameters.description:
* `pro` - Professional
* `non_pro` - Non-Professional

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Case-insensitive exact match on user email.

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: Sort by allowed fields only: `created`, `email`, `full_name`, `prop_firm__slug`, `user_type`. Supports single (`order_by=email`), descending (`order_by=-created`), and multi-column (`order_by=email,-created`).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: prop_firm_id
parameters.description: Comma-separated firm IDs where the user has an active membership.

parameters.name: prop_firm_slug
parameters.description: Comma-separated firm slugs where the user has an active membership.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive partial match across user `id`, `email`, and `full_name`.

parameters.name: user_type
parameters.description: Filter by self-cert classification. Supported values: `pro`, `non_pro`.

post.description: Create a trader user for your firm. The user can access only the trader dashboard and cannot access admin-only endpoints. If the email already belongs to an existing user, that user is granted access for your firm and the submitted password is ignored. Use `results[].existing_user` to decide whether to show the submitted onboarding password or tell the trader to use or reset their existing password.

CreateTraderUser.description: Example request to create a new trader user under the authenticated firm.

NewUserSuccessResponse.description: `existing_user=false` means the submitted password was applied to the newly created user.

ExistingUserSuccessResponse.summary: Existing user granted access for your firm
ExistingUserSuccessResponse.description: `existing_user=true` means the email already belongs to an existing user. The submitted password was ignored; direct the trader to use or reset their existing password.

value.detail: User creation failed: last name should not be empty.

ProvisioningValidationError.summary: Trader user could not be created
ProvisioningValidationError.description: Validation details are returned when safe to expose.

502.description: Provisioning failed; the user was not created. `upstream_detail` is included when a safe validation message is available.

get.description: Returns a single user with annotated account counts.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A unique integer value identifying this user.

patch.description: Allows authorized callers to update supported user profile/contact fields and KYC verification status. Use deactivate/reactivate endpoints to manage user access.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A unique integer value identifying this user.

200.description: Returns a confirmation object with `details` when the user details are updated.

get.description: Returns purchased, active and failed account totals for the user, aggregated across duplicate accounts in the same group. Cached for 5 minutes.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A unique integer value identifying this user.

post.description: Deactivate a user with an audit reason. By default, the user's trading accounts for your firm are also blocked. Set `deactivate_accounts=false` to leave trading accounts unchanged.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A unique integer value identifying this user.

get.description: Accepts a token key in the path (`/api/users/{token}/entitlements/`), resolves the token owner, and returns data entitlements for that user if eligible.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A unique integer value identifying this user.

200.description: Entitlements payload. Returns a data entitlement map when eligible; otherwise returns an empty entitlement map.

post.description: Restore access for a previously deactivated user. This endpoint reactivates only the user profile and does not change trading accounts.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A unique integer value identifying this user.

value.reason: User access restored after account review.

ReactivateUser.description: Example request for restoring access to a previously deactivated user.

patch.description: Update your firm's entitlement status for the requested user and entitlement package. L2 market data access may be created directly. L1 access can be updated only when the user already has L1 eligibility. Call `PATCH /api/users/{user_id}/entitlements/` using the `user_id` value returned by `GET /api/entitlements/user-entitlements/`, and pass either the entitlement name or exchange codes plus level in the request body to identify which package should be changed. Use `AUTHORIZED` to grant access, `REVOKED` to remove access, or `PENDING` to restore a pending state for your firm. Revoking access for your firm may not change the entitlement status shown by later entitlement reads if the user remains eligible through another source.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Trader user id whose entitlement records should be updated.

AuthorizeL2DataRequest.summary: Allow L2 data for a user

AuthorizeMultipleExchanges.summary: Allow L2 data for several exchanges

204.description: Your firm's targeted entitlement status was created or updated successfully.

404.description: The requested entitlement or exchange code was not found, or the user does not already have the requested L1 entitlement for your firm.

get.description: Returns a paginated per-user entitlement snapshot. Each user item includes every entitlement key; `true` means the user has at least one authorized entitlement that enables trading for that entitlement.

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: Sort by allowed fields only: `created`, `email`, `full_name`. Supports single (`order_by=email`), descending (`order_by=-created`), and multi-column (`order_by=email,-created`).

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive partial match across user `id`, `email`, and `full_name`.

get.description: Exports every user matching the same filters, search, sorting, permissions, and firm scope used by the users list. The CSV mirrors the users-list response fields except user-deduplication duplicate-group details.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Case-insensitive exact match on user email.

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: Sort by allowed fields only: `created`, `email`, `full_name`, `prop_firm__slug`, `user_type`. Supports single (`order_by=email`), descending (`order_by=-created`), and multi-column (`order_by=email,-created`).

parameters.name: prop_firm_id
parameters.description: Comma-separated firm IDs where the user has an active membership.

parameters.name: prop_firm_slug
parameters.description: Comma-separated firm slugs where the user has an active membership.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive partial match across user `id`, `email`, and `full_name`.

parameters.name: user_type
parameters.description: Filter by self-cert classification. Supported values: `pro`, `non_pro`.

get.description: List your firm's withdrawals from the last 365 days, with optional filtering by payout month, account ID, or both.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Specific account ID to return withdrawals for.

parameters.name: date_filter
parameters.description: Filter by month (ISO 8601 format) or "All"

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of items per page

400.description: Invalid page, page size, account ID, or date filter.

get.description: Return estimated payout details and the next withdrawal date for a user's account.

403.description: Withdrawal is not allowed for the requested account.

post.description: Submit a withdrawal request for the requested account. When accepted, the account is blocked from trading.
Authenticates a user with an email address and password.

200.description: Returns the issued session token and related login metadata.

post.description: Rotates a refresh token and returns a new session access token.

account_ids.description: Account ids to reactivate. Maximum 500 per request.

reason.description: Audit-log reason recorded against every reactivated account.

reset_balance.description: When true, also resets balance, equity and PnL to the account's initial balance.

user_id.description: ID of the user who will own the trading account.

challenge_id.description: Challenge or program identifier associated with the account. The first phase for the challenge is used.

phase_id.description: Optional exact phase identifier for the account. When provided, it must belong to the selected challenge. When omitted or null, the first phase for the challenge is used.

initial_balance.description: Optional account-level starting balance override. Precedence is explicit account initial_balance, then the selected Phase initial_balance, then the Challenge initial_balance.

is_active.description: Whether the account is active immediately after creation.

notes.description: Optional notes or comments to store with the account.

AccountDisabled.description: Compact payload returned by the disabled-account listing endpoint.

status.description:
Current terminal status for the account.

* `PENDING_ACTIVATION` - Pending Activation
* `ACTIVE` - Active
* `PASSED` - Passed
* `FAILED` - Failed
* `BLOCKED` - Blocked
* `DELETED` - Deleted
* `LIVE_VERIFICATION` - Live Account Verification Required
* `KYC_VERIFICATION_REQD` - KYC Verification Required
* `PHASE_VERIFICATION_REQD` - Phase Verification Required
* `PAUSED` - PAUSED
* `SUBSCRIPTION_EXPIRED` - Subscription Expired
* `PAYMENT_REQUESTED` - Payment Requested

disabled_at.description: Timestamp when the account entered its current disabled state.

reason.description: Custom disable reason when present, otherwise the stored status reason.

AccountSummary.description:
Reporting-oriented account payload that supports restql ``?query=`` field
selection and nested expansion. Pair with ``AccountViewSet.summary`` via
``ReportingViewSetMixin`` so eager-loading and conditional annotations
fire based on which fields the client requested.

Counts (long_positions_count, short_positions_count) are queryset
annotations attached by ``AccountSummaryService.annotate_counts`` only
when the requesting query asks for them. ``trading_days`` is precomputed
in bulk by ``compute_trading_days_bulk`` and attached to each instance
as ``_trading_days_cache`` to avoid per-row work.

daily_pnl.description: Profit or loss for the account during the current trading day.

realized_pnl.description: Realized profit or loss for the account.

unrealized_pnl.description: Unrealized profit or loss from currently open exposure.

total_pnl.description: Realized lifetime profit or loss for the account.

realized_lifetime_pnl.description: Realized lifetime profit or loss for the account.

total_commission.description: Total commission charged to the account.

peak_balance.description: Peak realized account balance for the relevant risk window, excluding open-position profit or loss.

peak_equity.description: Peak account equity for the relevant risk window, including open-position profit or loss.

peak_equity_timestamp.description: Timestamp when the peak account equity was recorded.

low_equity_timestamp.description: Timestamp when the lowest account equity was recorded.

min_balance.description: Lowest observed account balance for the relevant risk window.

failure_threshold.description: Failure threshold supplied with failure context, when available.

failure_metadata.description: Structured breach or failure context for the account, when available.

long_positions_count.description: Number of closed long positions for the account. All-time by default; scoped by `position_counts_closed_at_after` / `position_counts_closed_at_before` when provided.

short_positions_count.description: Number of closed short positions for the account. All-time by default; scoped by `position_counts_closed_at_after` / `position_counts_closed_at_before` when provided.

email.description: Email address of the account owner.

account_type.description: Type of trading account (e.g. challenge, verification, funded).

user_id.description: Unique identifier of the user who owns the trading account.

prop_firm_id.description: Unique identifier of the proprietary trading firm.

prop_firm_name.description: Display name of the proprietary trading firm.

challenge_title.description: Title of the challenge attached to the account's current phase.

challenge_short_code.description: Short code copied from the challenge attached to the account's current phase.

auto_liquidate_triggered_timestamp.description: Timestamp when auto-liquidation was triggered, if applicable.

failure_metadata.description: Structured breach or failure context for the account, when available.

current_equity.description: Current account equity, including unrealized profit or loss.

previous_end_of_day_balance.description: Account balance at the end of the previous trading day.

daily_pnl.description: Profit or loss for the account during the current trading day.

realized_pnl.description: Realized profit or loss for the account.

unrealized_pnl.description: Unrealized profit or loss from currently open exposure.

total_pnl.description: Realized lifetime profit or loss for the account.

realized_lifetime_pnl.description: Realized lifetime profit or loss for the account.

total_commission.description: Total commission charged to the account.

closed_pnl_today.description: Realized profit or loss from positions closed during the current trading day.

open_pnl.description: Legacy open-position profit or loss value. Prefer unrealized_pnl when present.

peak_realized_balance.description: Highest realized account balance reached for the relevant risk window.

peak_balance.description: Peak realized account balance for the relevant risk window, excluding open-position profit or loss.

peak_equity.description: Peak account equity for the relevant risk window, including open-position profit or loss.

peak_equity_timestamp.description: Timestamp when the peak account equity was recorded.

lowest_realized_balance.description: Lowest realized account balance reached historically.

peak_unrealized_balance.description: Highest account equity reached for the relevant risk window.

lowest_unrealized_balance.description: Lowest unrealized equity value reached historically.

low_equity_timestamp.description: Timestamp when the lowest account equity was recorded.

eod_peak_balance.description: End-of-day peak balance used for EOD trailing drawdown calculations.

failure_threshold.description: Failure threshold supplied with failure context, when available.

account_currency.description: Currency in which the trading account is denominated.

auto_liquidation_eNonebled.description: Indicates whether auto-liquidation is enabled for the account.

auto_liquidation_criteria.description: Criteria used to determine when auto-liquidation should be triggered.

auto_liquidation_triggered.description: Indicates whether auto-liquidation has been triggered.

auto_liquidation_timestamp.description: Timestamp when auto-liquidation occurred, if triggered.

net_positions_count.description: Net number of open positions (long minus short).

is_trading_allowed.description: Indicates whether the account is currently allowed to place trades.

oms_flags.description: Operational or risk flags reported for the account.

last_trade_timestamp.description: Timestamp of the most recent trade executed on the account.

risk_algorithm.description: Risk management algorithm currently applied to the account.

drawdown_type.description: Type of drawdown applied to the account (e.g. static, trailing).

max_drawdown.description: Maximum allowed drawdown for the account.

daily_max_drawdown.description: Maximum allowed daily drawdown for the account.

minimum_account_balance.description: Lowest observed account balance for the relevant risk window.

minimum_account_balance_timestamp.description: Timestamp when the lowest observed account balance was recorded, if applicable.

trading_days.description: Number of distinct trading days with at least one executed trade.

consistency_score.description: Score representing the trader's consistency based on risk and performance metrics.

end_date.description: Specified for Time Limited accounts. Leave blank to allow unlimited duration

min_balance.description: Lowest observed account balance for the relevant risk window.

BrokerTypeEnum.description:
* `ALL` - Do not filter
* `MatchTrade` - Match Trader
* `MetaTrader` - MetaTrader
* `TradeLocker` - TradeLocker
* `MetaTrader5` - MetaTrader5
* `Ctrader` - Ctrader
* `Sim2Funded` - Sim2Funded
* `Tradovate` - Tradovate
* `Volumetrica` - Volumetrica
* `Tickblaze` - Tickblaze
* `TickblazeOMS` - TickblazeOMS

short_code.description: Short code used by OMS and account surfaces for this challenge.

ClosedPositionShallow.description:
Closed-position shape for nested expansion on reporting endpoints.

Intentionally exposes no relation fields. ``direction`` and ``state``
are returned as their string labels (``Long``/``Short``, ``Closed``)
rather than internal numeric codes so reporting clients don't need a
lookup table.

CurrentStatus293Enum.description:
* `PENDING_ACTIVATION` - Pending Activation
* `ACTIVE` - Active
* `PASSED` - Passed
* `FAILED` - Failed
* `BLOCKED` - Blocked
* `DELETED` - Deleted
* `LIVE_VERIFICATION` - Live Account Verification Required
* `KYC_VERIFICATION_REQD` - KYC Verification Required
* `PHASE_VERIFICATION_REQD` - Phase Verification Required
* `PAUSED` - PAUSED
* `SUBSCRIPTION_EXPIRED` - Subscription Expired
* `PAYMENT_REQUESTED` - Payment Requested

data_level.description:
Market-data depth or package tier for this entitlement.

* `l1` - Level 1
* `l2` - Level 2

exchanges.description: Exchange codes covered by this entitlement.

GlobalUserEntitlement.description: Aggregate user entitlement with active-firm status context.

user_id.description: User id for the entitlement holder.

user_email.description: Email address of the user this entitlement applies to.

status.description:
Overall entitlement status for this user across eligible sources. `AUTHORIZED` takes precedence over `PENDING`; `PENDING` takes precedence over `REVOKED`.

* `PENDING` - Pending Authorization
* `AUTHORIZED` - Authorized
* `REVOKED` - Revoked

firm_status.description: Status of your firm's entitlement for this user. This can differ from `status` when the user remains eligible through another source.

created.description: Timestamp when this overall entitlement record was created.

timestamp.description: Time the health response was generated.

version.description: Application version when available; may be null when no version is reported.

KeyEnum.description:
* `self_cert_pro_status` - Self Certification Pro Status
* `self_cert_non_pro_status` - Self Certification Non-Pro Status
* `cme_subscriber_addendum` - CME Subscriber Addendum

prop_firm.description: Firm slug bound to the issued session token.

prop_firms.description: Firm slugs available to the authenticated user.

refresh_token.description: Refresh token for obtaining new access tokens

self_cert_status.description: Self certification statuses plus remaining forms that can still be signed.

OperationResponse.description:
Standard serializer for operation responses. A command or a task should return a response matching this format.
Operation = a request to perform some action, e.g. sync, update, delete, create, etc.

success.description: Indicates whether the operation completed successfully.

results.description: List containing the result items produced by the operation. The structure of each item depends on the executed operation.

summary.description: Aggregated summary information about the operation results (e.g. counts of updated, created, failed or unchanged records).

duration_ms.description: Execution time of the operation in milliseconds.

message.description: Optional human-readable message describing the operation outcome.

meta.description: Pagination metadata. Use `has_next`/`has_previous` for boolean page checks and `next`/`previous` for page URLs.

count.description: Total number of items matching the request.

page_size.description: Number of items returned per page.

has_next.description: Whether another page is available after this one.

has_previous.description: Whether a page is available before this one.

next.description: URL for the next page, or null on the final page.

previous.description: URL for the previous page, or null on the first page.

custom_status_reason.description: Optional note stored with the account status.

triggered_by.description: Optional actor label. Defaults to the authenticated user's email, name, or identifier, then 'system'.

user_id.description: Optional user identifier to associate with the close request. Defaults to the account owner.

triggered_by.description: Optional actor label. Defaults to the authenticated user's email, name, or identifier, then 'system'.

user_id.description: Optional user identifier to associate with the close request. Defaults to the account owner.

entitlement.description: Human-readable entitlement name to update for this user.

exchange_codes.description: Exchange codes whose matching entitlements should be updated for this user. Use this instead of `entitlement` when changing multiple exchanges in one request.

level.description:
Market data level for the entitlement being updated. L2 requests can create a new entitlement entry for your firm; L1 requests require existing eligibility for the requested entitlement.

* `l1` - Level 1
* `l2` - Level 2

status.description:
New status to apply to your firm's entitlement for the selected user and entitlement. This does not change eligibility from other sources.

* `PENDING` - Pending Authorization
* `AUTHORIZED` - Authorized
* `REVOKED` - Revoked

PayoutFrequencyEnum.description:
* `30` - Monthly
* `14` - Bi-Weekly
* `7` - Weekly
* `10` - Every 10 days
* `3` - Every 3 days
* `5` - Every 5 days
* `-1` - Triple Payout
* `21` - Every 21 days
* `28` - Every 28 days
* `1` - Daily

PhaseShallow.description:
Phase shape for nested expansion on reporting endpoints.

Surfaces the rule values that define the challenge phase the account is
on (target profit, drawdown limits, trading-day requirements, leverage).

trading_period_days.title: Total Trading Days
trading_period_days.description: Number of days before the account expires

initial_balance.description: Phase-level starting balance amount, or null when the challenge default applies.

trading_period_days.title: Total Trading Days
trading_period_days.description: Number of days before the account expires

profit_split.description: The fraction of available funds the user will receive.

page_size.description: Number of withdrawals returned per page.

documents.description: Withdrawals returned for the requested page.

average_payout.description: Average requested withdrawable amount across withdrawals from the last 365 days, scoped by account ID when provided; the optional month filter does not change this value.

ReconciliationStatusEnum.description:
* `not_reconciled` - Not reconciled
* `original_superseded` - Original superseded
* `replacement_account` - Replacement account

refresh_token.description: Refresh token to obtain a new access token

prop_firms.description: All active Prop Firm slugs the authenticated user belongs to.

status.description:
Current outage state. `ok` means no outage is reported; `scheduled_outage` is returned during the daily 17:00–18:00 America/New_York maintenance window; `unscheduled_outage` is reserved for unplanned outage reporting.

* `ok` - ok
* `scheduled_outage` - scheduled_outage
* `unscheduled_outage` - unscheduled_outage

declaration_type.description:
Declaration type accepted by `POST /api/users/self-cert/`.

* `pro` - Pro
* `non_pro` - Non-Pro
* `cme_subscriber_addendum` - CME Subscriber Addendum

available_forms_to_sign.description: Forms that still need action. When a declaration status is not `accepted`, it appears in this list.

SourceEnum.description:
* `WEBHOOK` - Webhook
* `ADMIN_MANUAL_CREATE` - Admin Manual Create
* `ADMIN_VERIFICATION` - Admin Verification
* `DATA_IMPORT` - Data Import

Status293Enum.description:
* `PENDING_ACTIVATION` - Pending Activation
* `ACTIVE` - Active
* `PASSED` - Passed
* `FAILED` - Failed
* `BLOCKED` - Blocked
* `DELETED` - Deleted
* `LIVE_VERIFICATION` - Live Account Verification Required
* `KYC_VERIFICATION_REQD` - KYC Verification Required
* `PHASE_VERIFICATION_REQD` - Phase Verification Required
* `PAUSED` - PAUSED
* `SUBSCRIPTION_EXPIRED` - Subscription Expired
* `PAYMENT_REQUESTED` - Payment Requested

StatusReasonEnum.description:
* `DAILY_DRAWDOWN_EXCEEDED` - Daily drawdown limit exceeded
* `MAX_DRAWDOWN_EXCEEDED` - Maximum drawdown limit exceeded
* `FLOATING_PNL_EXCEEDED` - Floating PNL limit exceeded
* `PROFIT_TARGET_REACHED` - Profit target completed
* `ACCOUNT_EXPIRED` - Account expired
* `TRADING_STRATEGY_NOT_SUITABLE` - Trading strategy does not suit our Organization
* `COPY_TRADING_DETECTED` - Copy trading detected
* `RULE_BREACHED` - Rule Breached
* `WITHDRAWAL_REQUESTED` - Withdrawal Requested
* `CHALLENGE_RESET` - Challenge Reset
* `SUBSCRIPTION_EXPIRED` - Subscription Expired

TickblazeProvisioningError.description: Error DTO for failed Tickblaze provisioning during trader creation.

detail.description: Human-readable provisioning failure returned by the API.

upstream_detail.description: Sanitized validation detail returned by Tickblaze when available.

firstPartyLicensedMics.description: Map of licensed MIC codes to entitlement levels for the token owner. Returns an empty object when the token owner is not eligible.

UserShallow.description: User shape for nested expansion on reporting endpoints.

UserTickblazeManager.description:
Serializer used by the Tickblaze Manager.

Exposes user fields relevant to account oversight.

user_type.description: Self-cert classification: `pro` when the pro declaration is accepted, otherwise `non pro`.

UserVenueTradingFlag.description:
Render a per-user venue snapshot.

The row resolves one representative Prop Firm so downstream systems can
identify the tenant without receiving the full firm payload.

profit_split.description: The fraction of available funds the user will receive.

withdrawal_amount.description: Optional withdrawable profit amount to request before applying the profit split. When omitted, the full available withdrawable amount is requested.
